name: vscode-browser-layout
description: Open browser tabs beside your code editor via a status bar button.

enumDescriptions: Open in the currently focused editor group (no layout management).
Open in the right editor group. Creates a two-group layout if needed and returns focus to the left group.
Open in the left editor group. Creates a two-group layout if needed and returns focus to the right group.
browserLayout.position.markdownDescription:
Where the Simple Browser tab opens.

- **active** — opens in whichever editor group is focused (default).
- **right** — ensures two editor groups and places the browser on the right, keeping your code on the left.
- **left** — ensures two editor groups and places the browser on the left, keeping your code on the right.

browserLayout.defaultUrl.markdownDescription:
A URL to open immediately when clicking the status bar button, **skipping the quick pick menu**. Leave empty to always show the quick pick.

Must start with `http://` or `https://`.

**Example:** `http://localhost:3000`

browserLayout.openOnStartup.markdownDescription: Automatically open a browser tab when the workspace loads. Uses `#browserLayout.defaultUrl#` as the URL. Has no effect if `defaultUrl` is empty.

browserLayout.statusBar.label.markdownDescription:
Text shown in the status bar button. Supports [codicons](https://microsoft.github.io/vscode-codicons/dist/codicon.html) like `$(globe)`, `$(browser)`, `$(link-external)`.

**Examples:**
- `$(globe) Browser` — icon + text (default)
- `$(globe)` — icon only
- `$(browser) Dev` — different icon + custom text
- *(empty)* — hides the status bar button entirely

browserLayout.urls.markdownDescription:
Preset URLs shown in the quick pick menu. The **Key** is the display label; the **Value** is the URL to open (must start with `http://` or `https://`).

**Example:**
```json
{
  "Local Dev": "http://localhost:3000",
  "Staging": "https://staging.example.com"
}
```

additionalProperties.markdownDescription: URL to open. Must start with `http://` or `https://`.

browserLayout.updateCheck.enabled.markdownDescription: Check [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/dcampman/vscode-browser-layout/releases) for new versions when VS Code starts. At most one check per `#browserLayout.updateCheck.intervalHours#`. You will always be asked before anything is installed.

browserLayout.updateCheck.intervalHours.markdownDescription: Minimum number of hours between update checks. Requires `#browserLayout.updateCheck.enabled#` to be `true`. Default is `24` (once per day).
